title: Salicornia australasica
taxonomy: Salicornia australasica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. It belongs to the genus Salicornia, which includes several species of succulent plants found in coastal areas. Salicornia australasica is native to Australia, where it grows in salt marshes and estuaries. This plant is often referred to as a saltwort or glasswort due to its ability to thrive in saline environments. The classification of Salicornia australasica reflects its unique adaptations to the harsh conditions found in coastal ecosystems.
